items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital improvement budget priorities
items.summary: Mountain View's City Council held a study session on April 14 to examine the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines planned infrastructure and development projects. This is the planning stage where residents can learn what physical improvements the city intends to fund in the coming year.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission introduced a new ordinance amending tenant protections in the city code. This could affect rental housing policies and renter rights in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: City releases youth services and library facilities status reports
items.summary: San José presented updates on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan and Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9. The reports include progress on youth empowerment programs and summer safety initiatives.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets renovation bonus opportunity
items.summary: A request to upgrade 405 Kipling Street's historic status could grant 5,000 additional square feet for rehabilitation, allowing the building to be modernized and seismically upgraded while preserving its historic character.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road
items.summary: A rezoning proposal would allow an 8-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 sq ft of commercial space on San Antonio Road. The project requires City Council approval and represents significant new housing near the commercial corridor.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: An Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for Wednesday, April 8 was cancelled, pushing back review of a project at 333 Franklin Street to a new date to be announced. Residents interested in this development will need to wait for a new hearing notice.

items.headline: City Council authorizes construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully specified in the agenda excerpt. Residents should monitor for follow-up information on what this construction project entails and where it will occur in Los Gatos.

items.headline: Mexican Heritage Plaza receives state arts and culture grant
items.summary: San José secured a California Natural Resources Agency grant for the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding supports cultural programming and facility development at the community institution.
